description: HP 5343A Microwave Frequency Counter (10 Hz to 26.5 GHz) with HP-IB Interface Option 011. Uses a function-code (program-code) remote language: each complete program code is executed by the microprocessor just as if entered from the front panel, so codes within a string must appear in the same order as front-panel entry (for example 'SR5AU'). Provides automatic/manual frequency mode, manual center frequency, frequency offset (including automatic offset), totalize mode, sweep mode, resolution and range selection, FM tolerance (acquisition time) selection, multiplication factor, four sample-rate (trigger/hold) modes, and output-mode control. Frequency measurement data is returned in a fixed-length ASCII format (SP F SP SP XXXXX.XXXXXX E 06 CR LF, in MHz) terminated by CR LF.

identity.description: The 5343A (HP-IB Option 011) program-code set contains no identification query and no SCPI '*IDN?'. When addressed to talk it returns only the measured frequency data, so there is no remote way to read back a model string.

commands.name: frequency_mode_auto
commands.description: Frequency Mode Select - Automatic. Selects the automatic frequency acquisition mode (analogous to the front panel AUTO operation).

commands.name: frequency_mode_manual
commands.description: Frequency Mode Select - Manual. Selects manual frequency mode; the center frequency is set with the SM (Set Manual Center Frequency) command.

commands.name: set_manual_center_frequency
commands.description: Set Manual Center Frequency. Sets the manual center frequency. The data string is a nonfixed-length string of up to 5 characters in MHz, terminated by E. Decimal points cause the entire string to be ignored; + signs and spaces are allowed. The number must be less than 26.5 GHz or it is ignored.

parameters.name: value
parameters.range: up to 5 characters, must be < 26500 (26.5 GHz); decimal points cause the string to be ignored

commands.name: frequency_offset_off
commands.description: Frequency Offset Mode Select - Off. Disables the frequency offset.

commands.name: frequency_offset_on
commands.description: Frequency Offset Mode Select - On. Enables the frequency offset set with the SOM command.

commands.name: set_frequency_offset
commands.description: Set Frequency Offset. Sets the frequency offset value. The data string is a nonfixed-length signed value in MHz terminated by E. Spaces are ignored.

commands.name: automatic_frequency_offset
commands.description: Automatic Frequency Offset. Sets the frequency offset automatically to the current measured value (zeroes the counter against the current reading). Allow time for a measurement to complete before sending SOMB, otherwise the in-progress measurement is cut off and the desired offset value is lost.

commands.name: totalize_mode_off
commands.description: Totalize Mode - Off. Turns the totalize (event count) mode off.

commands.name: totalize_mode_on
commands.description: Totalize Mode - On. Turns the totalize (event count) mode on. The input must be applied to the 10 Hz-520 MHz BNC only. To output in totalize, an output (ST) command must be enabled prior to enabling totalize; trigger commands (trg) are not effective in totalize. The only commands accepted in totalize are system commands such as CM0, CM1, CMC.

commands.name: totalize_mode_clear
commands.description: Totalize Mode - Clear. Clears the accumulated totalize count.

commands.name: sweep_mode_off
commands.description: Sweep Mode - Off. Turns the sweep mode off.

commands.name: sweep_mode_on
commands.description: Sweep Mode - On. Turns the sweep mode on. In sweep mode the SAMPLE RATE control has no effect and the 5343A awaits a trigger from an external source via the rear-panel SWP INTFC A connector. All commands are accepted except Manual (M), Set Manual Center Frequency (SM), and Sample Rate (T0-T3).

commands.name: resolution
commands.description: Resolution. Selects the measurement resolution. SR3=1 Hz, SR4=10 Hz, SR5=100 Hz, SR6=1 kHz, SR7=10 kHz, SR8=100 kHz, SR9=1 MHz. (Note: SR1 is the separate Check Mode code.)

commands.name: range_low
commands.description: Range Select - Low. Selects the 10 Hz to 500 MHz range.

commands.name: range_high
commands.description: Range Select - High. Selects the 500 MHz to 26.5 GHz range.

commands.name: fm_tolerance_short
commands.description: FM Tolerance (Acquisition Time) - 6 MHz p-p FM (Fast or Short Time). Selects the fast acquisition time, tolerating up to 6 MHz peak-to-peak FM.

commands.name: fm_tolerance_medium
commands.description: FM Tolerance (Acquisition Time) - 20 MHz p-p FM (Medium Time). Selects the medium acquisition time, tolerating up to 20 MHz peak-to-peak FM.

commands.name: fm_tolerance_long
commands.description: FM Tolerance (Acquisition Time) - 50 MHz p-p FM (Slow or Long Time). Selects the slow acquisition time, tolerating up to 50 MHz peak-to-peak FM.

commands.name: set_multiplication_factor
commands.description: Set Multiplication Factor. Sets the display multiplication factor. The data string is up to two characters terminated by E.

parameters.name: value
parameters.range: data string of up to two characters (e.g. 2, 34)

commands.name: select_dac
commands.description: Select Digital-to-Analog Converter (Option 004). Selects the front-panel character position (1 to 9, position 1 is the left-most digit) for the DAC; the selected digit and the two to its right are converted.

commands.name: sample_rate_front_panel
commands.description: Sample Rate Mode - Front panel. Sample rate is determined by the front panel SAMPLE RATE control.

commands.name: sample_rate_hold
commands.description: Sample Rate Mode - Hold. Places the counter in hold; a trigger message (e.g. trg 7) must be sent to start a measurement. If the 5343A is in remote and addressed to listen and not already in Hold (T1), a trigger command automatically puts it into Sample-then-Hold (T3).

commands.name: sample_rate_fast
commands.description: Sample Rate Mode - Fast sample (no delay). The counter ignores any sample-rate run-down and initiates a new measurement as soon as the previous measurement is over.

commands.name: sample_rate_sample_then_hold
commands.description: Sample Rate Mode - Sample then Hold. The counter takes a measurement and holds until the next T3 or trigger message.

commands.name: output_mode_no_output
commands.description: Output Mode - No output to bus. The counter does not output measurement data to the bus.

commands.name: output_mode_only_when_addressed
commands.description: Output Mode - Output only when addressed. At the end of a measurement the counter pulls SRQ and checks if it has been addressed to talk; if not, it clears SRQ and starts the next measurement. If addressed to talk it outputs the measurement, clears SRQ, and starts the next measurement.

commands.name: output_mode_wait_until_addressed
commands.description: Output Mode - Wait until addressed. At the end of a measurement the counter pulls SRQ and waits in a loop until addressed to talk, then outputs the measurement, clears SRQ, and starts the next measurement. Use this mode when triggering in HOLD (T1) and then addressing to talk.

commands.name: reset
commands.description: Reset. Blanks the display and initiates a new measurement. If in Hold (T1) the measurement is not completed but stays in Hold. Does not return control to local.

commands.name: power_up_conditions
commands.description: Power-Up Conditions. Returns the counter to its power-up state: Auto mode, 1 Hz resolution, and resets all special functions (keyboard lock, sweep, multiplication factor to 1). Sample rate and output modes are not under P-code control.

commands.name: check_mode
commands.description: Check Mode. Internal check function. No input may be present at the RF connector. Send the RESET command (R) before making other measurements.
